title: India conducts naval firing exercises in Arabian Sea amid Pakistan tensions
summary: CNN Breaking News reports the Indian Navy conducting live firing exercises in the Arabian Sea off the Gujarat coast, maintaining elevated readiness despite the ceasefire with Pakistan.
contents:
# Indian Navy Conducts Live Firing Exercises in Arabian Sea

## Exercise Details
The Indian Navy conducted live firing exercises in designated areas of the Arabian Sea off the Gujarat and Maharashtra coasts on May 1, 2026. Maritime notices warned commercial shipping of firing zones active through the day.

## Forces Involved
- Indian Navy surface combatants conducting gunnery exercises
- Maritime patrol aircraft providing surveillance coverage
- Coastal defense units on heightened alert along the western seaboard

## Strategic Context
The exercises occur within the framework of the India-Pakistan ceasefire but demonstrate continued Indian military readiness. Gujarat shares a maritime boundary with Pakistan, and the Arabian Sea serves as a potential theater for naval confrontation.

## Diplomatic Significance
Defense analysts note the exercises serve dual purposes: maintaining combat readiness and signaling to Islamabad that Indian forces remain prepared despite the ceasefire framework.

## Tags
india, navy, arabian-sea, firing-exercises, pakistan-tensions, military-readiness
